# Preface {.unnumbered}

This book teaches you how GPT-style language models actually work, from the very first step — turning text into integers — all the way to the final step — turning vectors back into words.

The code language is Python 3.
Not because it is the only possible choice, but because Python is what the field runs on, and because the math translates almost directly to syntax.
NumPy makes a matrix a matrix.
A function is a formula.
When you read `np.matmul(W, x) + b`, you are reading **Wx + b** with different punctuation.
Other languages would work.
This one requires the least translation.

Every figure in this book is generated by a matplotlib script, living alongside the prose in `src/figures/`.
You can reproduce any diagram by running the corresponding script.

By the end, you will have a complete working miniature GPT — a minimal but correct GPT implementation in Python that you can run and inspect.
Not a toy metaphor: actual matrix multiplications, actual softmax, actual multi-head attention.
